title: brainstorming

goal.text: Run the design-first brainstorming workflow before any creative implementation work and preserve its approval gates.

requirements.summary: Turn an idea into an approved design and written spec before any implementation work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: Project context was explored before design discussion.
Visual companion branching was handled correctly when visual explanation was relevant.
Clarifying questions were asked one at a time.
At least 2-3 approaches were presented before settling on a design.
The design was approved before writing the spec.
The written spec was reviewed by both the agent and the user before handoff.
The workflow ends by transitioning to writing-plans instead of implementation.

tasks.title: Explore context and choose the brainstorming path
tasks.detail:
Inspect the current project context first, then decide whether this request fits a single brainstorming round or must be decomposed before continuing. Also decide whether the visual companion branch is likely to matter.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/brainstorming/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the canonical workflow order and branch rules.

single-scope.summary: The request fits one brainstorming round.
nextsteps: Offer the visual companion only if future questions are visual.

decompose-first.summary: The request needs decomposition before detailed brainstorming.
nextsteps: Decompose the larger effort into sub-projects first.
Then return to the first sub-project and continue the same brainstorming workflow.

tasks.title: Run discovery, optioning, and design approval
tasks.detail:
Ask clarifying questions one at a time, optionally offer the visual companion in its own message when visuals would help, then present 2-3 approaches and a recommended design. Present the design in sections and wait for approval before proceeding.

Do not invoke implementation skills or produce code during this task.

default.summary: The design is approved; proceed to the written-spec phase.
nextsteps: Write the spec under docs/superpowers/specs with the expected naming pattern.
Keep the original brainstorming constraints active until the written spec review gate passes.

tasks.title: Write the spec, self-review it, and get user review
tasks.detail: Write the approved design doc, run the self-review loop for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, and scope, then ask the user to review the written spec before moving on.

approved.summary: The written spec is approved and ready for planning.
nextsteps: Transition to writing-plans as the only allowed next skill.
Do not start implementation from brainstorming.

revise-spec.summary: The spec needs changes before planning.
nextsteps: Repeat self-review and user review before handoff.

tasks.title: Handoff to writing-plans and verify no implementation work leaked in
tasks.detail: Confirm the brainstorming workflow ended cleanly: no implementation happened early, the spec was approved, and the next move is invoking writing-plans rather than coding or another implementation skill.

references.why: Preserves the ordered workflow, branches, gates, and helper inventory of the converted brainstorming skill.
Defines the optional visual-companion branch and its message-shape constraints.
Supports the written-spec review phase after the design is documented.
Keeps the full original superpowers wording, examples, and rationale beside the claw entry.

rules: Do not start implementation, invoke implementation skills, or scaffold code before design approval.
Ask one clarifying question per message.
Offer the visual companion only as its own message and only when visuals would materially help.
Present 2-3 approaches before settling on a design.
After the written spec is approved, the only intended handoff is writing-plans.
Use SUPERPOWERS-FALLBACK.md when exact source wording or nuance matters.
